title: Pouvoirs de signature du Royaume-Uni
text_preview: Whereas, for the better treating of and arranging certain matters which have come into discussion between the Government of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland and the Governments of other Powers and States relative to the International Institute of Refrigeration, it is expedient that a fit person should be invested with Full Power on the part of the Government of the United Kingdom of Great Bri…

pages.text:
Whereas, for the better treating of and arranging certain matters which have come into discussion between the

Government of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern

Ireland and the Governments of other Powers and States relative to the International Institute of Refrigeration, it is expedient that a fit person should be invested with Full Power on the part of the Government of the United Kingdom of Great

Britain and Northern Ireland: I, Sir Anthony Eden, Knight

Companion of the Most Noble Order of the Garter, a Member of

Her Majesty's Most Honourable Privy Council, &c., &c., Her

Majesty's Principal Secretary Of State for Foreign Affairs, do hereby certify that Sir Hubert Miles Gladwyn Jebb, Knight

Grand Cross of the Most Distinguished Order of Saint Michael and Saint George, Companion of the Most Honourable Order of the

Bath, Her Majesty's Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipoten- tiary at Paris, is by these presents named, constituted and appointed as Plenipotentiary and Representative having Full

Power and Authority to conclude with such Plenipotentiaries or

Representatives as may be vested with similar Power and

Authority on the part of the Governments of other Powers and

States as aforesaid the Agreement concerning the International

Institute of Refrigeration, which was opened for signature at

Paris on the First day of December, One thousand Nine hundred and Fifty-four, and to sign for the Government of the United

Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, everything so concluded. Further, I do hereby certify that whatever things shall be so concluded by the said Plenipotentiary and Represen- tative, shall, subject if necessary to Ratification by the

Government

Government of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern

Ireland, be agreed to, acknowledged and accepted by the said

Government of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern

Ireland in the fullest manner.

In witness whereof I have signed these Presents and affixed hereto my Seal.

Signed and sealed at the Foreign Office, London, the

Seventeenth day of February, One thousand Nine hundred and

Fifty-five.

Are They Iden
